title: camellia sinensis subsp. buisanensis
intro: Camellia sinensis subsp. buisanensis is a subspecies of the Camellia sinensis plant, commonly referred to as a wild tea plant or Taiwanese wild tea. It is a medicinal plant known for its therapeutic properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including high blood pressure, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to promote cardiovascular health, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Camellia sinensis subsp. buisanensis is often prepared as a tea, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed on its own or used as a base for herbal blends to support overall well-being.
